repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body: As part of PR, the importing of the shared module was moved to earlier stage to allow some initializing functions to be shared.

comment_data.body:
`PyType_Ready` won't work if you mix modules with `CYTHON_USE_TYPE_SPECS` defined with modules without (most likely Stable ABI and not stable ABI).

I don't think that's a problem - it isn't the only place we have that limitation and it'd be a very hard limitation to avoid even if we tried.

However, I do think we should make sure it's documented: see #7571
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -114,7 +114,7 @@ static int __Pyx_validate_bases_tuple(const char *type_name, int has_dictoffset,
 #endif
 
 
-/////////////// PyType_Ready.proto ///////////////
+/////////////// PyType_Ready.export ///////////////

commit.message: Fix shared_utility_qualified_name in CythonUtilityCodeContext and NonManglingModuleScope
Run tests with shared module enabled
Skip tests which are not related to shared utility module
Generate utility code in subprocess instead of calling directly function
Generate shared utility module onle once per test run
Allow CYTHON_UNUSED macro in shared function

title: Move more code to shared utility code
